subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand why people are considered a resource
Explain the role of education and health in human capital formation
Distinguish between economic (market) and non-economic (non-market) activities
Identify the primary, secondary, and tertiary sectors of the economy
Learn about the types of unemployment in India

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Human Capital
topics_covered: Concept of Human Capital vs. Physical Capital
How investment in humans yields returns
teaching_strategy: Introductory discussion using the 'Story of Sakal and Vilas'
student_activities: Identify assets in their own family (education/skills)
Read and summarize the contrast between Sakal and Vilas
assessment: Oral questioning on the definition of Human Capital

lesson_plan.title: Investment in Education
topics_covered: Role of education in individual and national growth
Government initiatives: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan and Mid-day Meals
teaching_strategy: Analysis of literacy rate graphs from the textbook
Discussion on the 'Virtuous Cycle' of education
student_activities: Draw a simple bar graph of literacy trends
Discuss how school helps in future job prospects
assessment: Short quiz on government education schemes

lesson_plan.title: Investment in Health
topics_covered: Importance of health in realizing one's potential
Improvement in Life Expectancy and Infant Mortality Rate (IMR)
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the 'Asset vs. Liability' concept of health
student_activities: Write a paragraph on 'Why a healthy worker is more productive'

lesson_plan.title: Economic Activities: Three Sectors
teaching_strategy: Visual sorting activity on the board
student_activities: Classify a list of 10 jobs into the three sectors
Draw a diagram representing the three sectors

lesson_plan.title: Market vs. Non-Market Activities
teaching_strategy: Discussion on why domestic work is not counted in National Income
Comparison of earnings based on education/skill
student_activities: Categorize daily tasks of parents as Market or Non-Market
Debate on the recognition of household labor

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Unemployment
teaching_strategy: Demonstration of 'Disguised Unemployment' using a role-play
student_activities: Identify types of unemployment in provided case studies
Draw a mind map of unemployment types

lesson_plan.title: Chapter Review and Wrap-up
teaching_strategy: Mind map on the board for the whole chapter

teacher_tip: Focus on the 'Virtuous Cycle'—how one generation's education leads to the next—to help students understand the long-term value of schooling.
